visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a graphic title card overlaid on a background of blurry white and pink roses. At the top is the red and blue Costco Wholesale logo. To the left is a small circular icon featuring a cartoon character from Wreck-It Ralph. The central text is stylized with pink and green outlines. (2) The scene transitions into a fast-paced sequence of still and panning shots of various product displays within a large warehouse retail store. Products are shown in bulk packaging on metal industrial shelves. Items include stacks of purple Kleenex tissue boxes, yellow boxes of Arm & Hammer cat litter, and packages of raw meat in a refrigerated case. (3) The sequence continues showing a variety of snacks and food items including Snickers minis, almond crisps, dried meat snacks, chocolate rolls, and jars of tropical fruit candy. Each shot features a prominent text overlay in the center detailing the product name and discount information. (4) Further products are showcased including large bags of blue Calbee pea crisps, Pringles boxes, Glico biscuit boxes, and Godiva chocolate boxes. The camera mostly uses static shots of the product stacks, occasionally with a slight handheld shake. (5) The final segments display fresh produce and refrigerated goods such as large bags of black grapes, blueberries, potatoes, and bunches of spring onions. The lighting remains bright and consistent with a warehouse environment. (6) The video concludes with shots of bottled drinks, milk, yogurt, and large trays of baked goods like cinnamon rolls and fish fillets, maintaining the same rapid editing pace and informational overlay style.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: No real people are visible in the video; it focuses exclusively on product displays and text graphics.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The background consistently features the interior of a Costco warehouse store. It includes high-stack industrial metal shelving units filled with bulk quantities of consumer goods, refrigerated open-top displays for meats and produce, and brightly lit aisles typical of a large-scale retail environment.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a high-energy, fast-paced male narrator speaking in Mandarin, introducing the 'Costco Weekly Specials'. The background music is a bright, upbeat electronic pop track with a strong 4/4 beat. (2) As each product appears on screen, the narrator rapidly lists the product name, original price, and discounted sale price. The vocal delivery is sharp and enthusiastic, with a rising intonation at the end of many sentences to maintain excitement. (3) Subtle 'pop' and 'ding' sound effects are interspersed precisely at the moment price figures appear on screen, enhancing the information delivery. (4) The narrator maintains this high speech rate and arousal level throughout the entire 87 seconds, with almost no silences. The background music remains constant in volume and tempo, providing a high-energy rhythmic bed for the narration. (5) The mix is clean with the vocal positioned clearly above the background music, indicating professional studio-level post-production.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Systematic Showcase -> Implicit CTA.
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To quickly inform consumers about a wide range of weekly discounted products at Costco Taiwan, particularly highlighting the Taoyuan Nankan branch. The high-energy audio and fast visual pace are designed to grab attention and convey a sense of 'don't-miss' urgency for these time-limited deals.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video is entirely focused on promoting specific products and prices at a named retail chain (Costco).

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video employs a typical 'social commerce' format optimized for vertical mobile viewing, using high-density text and rapid-fire audio to deliver maximum information in a short duration. The specific mention of the 'Taoyuan Nankan Store' for certain items suggests localized promotional targeting.
